markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 199 **Aspect** Emotion --- **Activate** command; **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** A surge of emotion emanates from your relic, granting a +1 status bonus to skill checks determined by the relic’s emotion. This bonus lasts for 1 minute.
**Anger** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks
**Awe** [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks
**Dedication** Checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367)
**Fear** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks
**Hatred** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks
**Joy** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks
**Love** [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) checks
**Misery** [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks
**Pride** [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) checks
**Remorse** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks
name: Emotion Surge

<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Fervor](/Relics.aspx?ID=205)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 199

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Emotion

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You gain the emotional fervor benefits for your relic’s [emotional state](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=97) for the next 3 rounds, but ignore the spell associated with the fervor. The feeling wears off quickly, so you experience no emotional fallout.
name: Fervor

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Emotion Conduit](/Relics.aspx?ID=206)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 199

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Emotion

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;     **Effect** You channel your relic’s emotion at a target within 30 feet, overpowering an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) effect on that creature. The relic attempts a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check. If the check succeeds, the target feels a sudden rush of the relic’s emotion, and the counteracted effect ends on the target. The target is temporarily immune to your relic’s emotion conduit until you make your next [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575).
name: Emotion Conduit

<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Fervent Spell](/Relics.aspx?ID=207)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Emotion

</row>

</column>

---

While the relic’s fervor gift lasts, you have access to the spell provided by the emotional state, which you can cast using your own spell slots. You also have access to the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command, Interact;     **Effect** The relic Casts the Spell associated with its emotional fervor at the spell’s lowest level.
name: Fervent Spell

<title level="1" right="Relic Grand Gift">[Quickening Emotion](/Relics.aspx?ID=208)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 199

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Emotion

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;   **Trigger** You are the target of an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) effect from a [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=80) source;  **Effect** The relic attempts a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check. If the check succeeds, the relic absorbs the effect completely, negating it. Instead, you’re [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for up to 1 minute, but no longer than the triggering effect’s duration. You then become temporarily immune to this effect for 1 hour.
name: Quickening Emotion

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 199

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Luck

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You attempt a d20 roll you’re untrained in;  **Effect** You treat your proficiency bonus as equal to half your level, or if the relic is 7th level or higher, as equal to your level. Then, roll twice, taking the better of the two results. This doesn’t allow you to use a skill’s trained actions.
name: Beginner's Luck

<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />

---

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You roll a failure or critical failure on a saving throw against an effect with multiple targets;  **Effect** You swap rolls with another target who’s willing to swap with you. Each of you determines the new results with your new rolls.
name: Swap Luck

name: Whammy

<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Steal Luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=213)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Contingency" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=385" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 199

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Luck

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You or a creature within 30 feet of you rolls two d20s for a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) or [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect and you like the higher roll;  **Effect** You steal the higher of the two numbers the triggering target rolled; the target must use the other result. Until you make your next [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), you can substitute that number for one d20 roll made for a saving throw or skill check you have to make against an effect created by an enemy, hazard, or the environment. Doing so is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect. If you fail to do so before your next daily preparations, you lose the stored number.
name: Steal Luck

<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Borrow Time](/Relics.aspx?ID=215)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 200

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Time

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;     **Effect** You point the relic at a creature within 30 feet, imbuing it with urgency. The creature gains the [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) condition for 1 round. After the condition ends, the creature is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round and temporarily immune to this gift until you next perform your daily preparations.
name: Borrow Time

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 200 **Aspect** Time --- **Activate** command, Interact; **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You target one creature within 30 feet, temporarily imposing the decrepitude of time on it. The target must attempt a Fortitude saving throw. The [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) conditions last 1 minute. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target becomes [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1.
**Failure** The target becomes clumsy 2, enfeebled 2, and [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1.
**Critical Failure** The target becomes clumsy 3, enfeebled 3, and drained 2.
name: Sands of the Hourglass

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Precognition](/Relics.aspx?ID=217)</title>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You analyze diverging time streams to determine the best course of action. Resolve two turns as if they occurred, then choose which one happens. The other does not occur.
name: Precognition

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 200 **Aspect** Time --- **Activate** envision; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You make time shudder around you, stealing it from enemies and granting it to allies. Creatures of your choice within 30 feet must attempt a Will save. For each creature you render slowed with this gift, you can render one ally within 30 feet, including you, [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 round. **Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1.
**Critical Failure** The creature is slowed 2.
name: Temporal Pulse

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Intelligent Relic](/Relics.aspx?ID=221)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 200

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Intelligent Relic

</row>

</column>

---

Your relic becomes an intelligent item. It has an alignment within one step of yours on each axis if not matching yours. It gains an alignment trait matching its alignment. The relic possesses precise sight at a range of 30 feet and imprecise hearing at a range of 30 feet. It is trained in Perception, increasing its proficiency when you increase yours. Choose whether it can speak aloud or communicate with you telepathically. It knows languages fitting its backstory, usually from among those you know. The relic is also trained in three skills, increasing one skill’s proficiency each time the relic gains a new gift. Your relic’s mental ability scores begin at +3, +2, and +1, arranged as you choose. The relic is also trained in Will saves, and its proficiency increases as yours does. Each time you gain a new gift, increase one of the relic’s mental ability scores by 2 or two by 1. The relic otherwise functions as normal for an intelligent item.
name: Intelligent Relic

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 198

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Emotion

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You speak at length about or demonstrate your craft, or you enact a performance. The relic casts [_enthrall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516) at a level equal to half its own. If you have legendary proficiency in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) or [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45), creatures can’t critically succeed on their saves against this spell. You can use [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) an Activation to sustain the effect as you would the spell.
name: Enthralling
